label: S01 · Bwaka 1999
timing: Acute/post-acute (2wk–2mo)
caveat: General case series; ocular data on 19 survivors, no formal eye exam; N/exam/examiner in original table were wrong.
author: Bwaka

rob_detail.notes: Q6/Q7 scored 'No': no ophthalmic examination (self-report/none) — ocular condition not validly/reliably measured

label: S02 · Kaweesa 2025
timing: Long-term (>2y)
caveat: SUDV, Gulu 2000; symptom survey, no eye exam.
author: Kaweesa

label: S03 · Clark 2015
timing: Multiple timepoints
caveat: 2007 Bundibugyo (BDBV); no eye exam (self-report symptoms). Original "Zaire/case series/N=103" was scrambled from Bwaka 1999 (its ref 4).
author: Clark

label: S04 · Shantha 2022
timing: Long-term (>2y)
caveat: Paediatric; 23 survivors/46 eyes; species not named in paper; original "Gulu 2000" note was mis-copied.
author: Shantha

label: S05 · Amuzu 2021
timing: Survivor (>6m)
caveat: SL 2013-16 paediatric survivors; self-report only. Species was wrongly BDBV; N 206→159; "Uganda 2007" note was a scrambled citation.
author: Amuzu

label: S06 · Balendra 2023
timing: Survivor (>6m)
caveat: Uveitis case series (128 uveitis patients); recruited 2022; examiner was ophthalmologist (original said non-specialist).
author: Balendra

label: S07 · Bates 2024
timing: Long-term (>2y)
caveat: Self-report survey, ZERO eye exams; original table listed slit-lamp/OCT/SUN in error.
author: Bates

rob_detail.notes: Q6/Q7 scored 'No': no ophthalmic examination (self-report/none) — ocular condition not validly/reliably measured

label: S08 · Choo 2024
timing: Long-term (>2y)
caveat: 521 survivors examined ~3.5y post-EVD; uveitis subtypes denominated among 69 uveitis cases.
author: Choo

label: S09 · Diallo 2021
timing: Long-term (>2y)
caveat: Guinea PostEboGui 802; ocular sequelae as composite category (non-specialist), no specific uveitis extractable.
author: Diallo

label: S10 · Etard 2017
timing: At enrolment (median 350 days, IQR 223–491 after ETC discharge)
caveat: Guinea PostEboGui; trained clinicians (not ophthalmologists); iridocyclitis as uveitis proxy.
author: Etard

label: S11 · Kelly 2022
timing: Baseline (median 317 days, IQR 271–366 from EVD onset) and 12-month follow-up visits
caveat: PREVAIL III secondary analysis; 991 survivors (3679 counted all contacts); 889 had baseline eye exam.
author: Kelly

label: S12 · LaVergne 2020
timing: Participants surveyed for PES every 6 months post-discharge; exact timing of blood/PBMC collection relative to EVD onset not stated
caveat: Immunological study; PES defined by self-reported symptoms; no ocular exam.
author: LaVergne

label: S13 · Schindell 2024
timing: 6-8 years post-recovery
caveat: Self-report survey; 595 survivors (998 counted contacts); no clinical eye exam.
author: Schindell

label: S14 · Shantha 2021
timing: Median 19 months (Phase 1) and 34 months (Phase 2) post-EVD diagnosis
caveat: EVICT surgical-selection cohort (cataract surgery); not a prevalence sample.
author: Shantha

rob_detail.notes: Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be MINORS; apply the proper MINORS instrument

label: S15 · Steptoe 2020
timing: Multiple timepoints: baseline (~13.1 months post-discharge) and follow-up (~25.6 months post-discharge)
caveat: 72 attended baseline, 57 analysed; symptomatic-referral cohort; species not named.
author: Steptoe

label: S16 · Taraborelli 2023
timing: Not reported
caveat: EVICT surgical-selection sub-analysis.
author: Taraborelli

rob_detail.notes: Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be MINORS; apply the proper MINORS instrument

label: S17 · Tiffany 2016
timing: Post-EVD; mean 51.1 days (SD 41.2) from ETC discharge to first follow-up visit; clinic period February–June 2015
examiner: Medical doctors at MSF clinic for initial VA assessment; ophthalmologists at Lowell and Ruth Gess UMC Eye Hospital for formal ocular diagnosis
caveat: 166 enrolled, 97 referred/examined; species not named; RoB upgraded from Low.
author: Tiffany

label: S18 · Fitzgerald 2018
timing: During acute EVD illness at Ebola holding unit admission (August 2014–March 2015); not survivor follow-up
caveat: Paediatric acute-triage/case-definition study (N=1054 acute admissions); NOT an ocular/survivor study; conjunctivitis was a triage symptom only.
author: Fitzgerald

rob_detail.notes: Q6/Q7 scored 'No': no ophthalmic examination (self-report/none) — ocular condition not validly/reliably measured

label: S19 · Guetiya 2017
timing: MHC operated February 7, 2015 to June 10, 2016 (16 months); data collection initiated 9 months after outbreak began and ended 26 months later (7 months after Sierra Leone declared Ebola-free by WHO)
examiner: Non-specialist (trained nurses and clinical officers; serious ocular cases referred to eye clinic)
caveat: Mobile clinic, nurses (no slit-lamp); paper states uveitis frequency could not be determined.
author: Guetiya

label: S20 · Mattia 2016
timing: Early (1-6m)
caveat: Port Loko 277 cohort; early convalescence (~4m); examiner not stated.
author: Mattia

label: S21 · Wing 2018
timing: median 109 days (IQR 91–210) from ETC discharge to first survivor clinic visit
examiner: General clinician (non-ophthalmologist); specialist referral arranged for those with relevant symptoms
caveat: Self-reported symptoms (7-day recall); no formal ophthalmic exam.
author: Wing

label: S22 · Eghrari 2021
timing: Long-term (›2y)
caveat: Cataract-surgery cohort (25 survivors+12 controls); original N_Enrolled 277 was wrong.
author: Eghrari

rob_detail.notes: Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be MINORS; apply the proper MINORS instrument

label: S23 · Fashina 2024
timing: March 2015 to March 2016 (EVICT study screening period); exact time from ETU discharge not stated
caveat: EVICT selection (active uveitis / cataract-surgery candidates); corneal findings among affected eyes, not survivors.
author: Fashina

label: S24 · Fashina 2023
timing: Not stated in abstract; median ETU stay 21 +/- 18.4 days
examiner: Ophthalmologist (comprehensive ophthalmic exam at Lowell and Ruth Gess Kissy Eye Hospital, Freetown)
caveat: Conference abstract; same EVICT cohort as Fashina 2024 (S23); selection bias.
author: Fashina

label: S25 · Green 2016
timing: median 142 days (IQR 127–159) from ETU discharge to specimen collection
caveat: EBOV RNA-persistence study; no ophthalmic exam, no ocular diagnoses.
author: Green

rob_detail.notes: Q6/Q7 scored 'No': no ophthalmic examination (self-report/none) — ocular condition not validly/reliably measured | Overall RoB reflects general study conduct; study reports no per-survivor ocular disease outcomes and is not pooled

label: S26 · Hereth 2017
timing: Survivor (>6m)
examiner: Ophthalmologist (eye investigator at ophthalmic departments of Donka National Hospital and Nzérékoré Regional Hospital; photographs reviewed by uveitis specialist)
caveat: Guinea PostEboGui; uveitis subtypes among 46 uveitis cases; original "abstract of Fashina" note was wrong.
author: Hereth

label: S27 · Howlett 2018
timing: Study conducted February 4–May 10, 2016; preliminary clinic attendees seen median 430 days (IQR 401–473) after first positive diagnostic result
caveat: Neurology-selected; ophthalmology in only 17/361; RoB upgraded from Low.
author: Howlett

label: S28 · Jagadesh 2018
timing: Recruited at ≥12 months post-discharge; median 18 months post-discharge at time of study (June 2016)
caveat: Case-control; 27 survivors (81 counted controls); self-report disability questionnaire, no eye exam.
author: Jagadesh

rob_detail.notes: Q6/Q7 scored 'No': no ophthalmic examination (self-report/none) — ocular condition not validly/reliably measured | Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be NOS; apply the proper NOS instrument | Overall RoB reflects general study conduct; study reports no per-survivor ocular disease outcomes and is not pooled

label: S29 · Kangbai 2019
timing: Acute phase during EVD treatment at ETC admission (June 2014 to April 2015); no post-discharge follow-up
caveat: Acute paediatric admissions (2014-15); conjunctivitis 20/139 as admission symptom; no eye exam.
author: Kangbai

label: S30 · Kulkarni 2026
timing: Survivor (>6m)
caveat: PREVAIL III baseline; 754 counted controls; paediatric subgroup analysed.
author: Kulkarni

label: S31 · Mattia 2016
timing: Post-discharge; uveitis symptoms onset median 3 weeks (IQR 0.4-8.6 weeks; range 0-17.2 weeks) after discharge from Ebola treatment centre; time to ophthalmologic assessment not stated
caveat: ARVO abstract; duplicate of S20 (Port Loko 277).
author: Mattia

label: S32 · Mudalegundi 2023
timing: Baseline at median ~11 months post symptom onset; annual follow-up for up to 60 months (5 visits)
caveat: PREVAIL longitudinal IOP study; uveitis listed only as IOP risk factor.
author: Mudalegundi

label: S33 · Shantha 2017
timing: Survivor (>6m)
caveat: ELWA 96 survivors; original "277 participants" note was mis-copied.
author: Shantha

label: S34 · Shantha 2019
timing: 2015 (post-acute EVD survivor clinic at ELWA Hospital)
caveat: ELWA clinic methods paper; duplicate of S33 (96 survivors).
author: Shantha

label: S35 · Steptoe 2018
timing: January 2016 to April 2017
caveat: 14 eyes selected for the Ebola retinal lesion; not representative.
author: Steptoe

label: S36 · Steptoe 2017
timing: Survivor (>6m; ~13.5m)
examiner: Local ophthalmic clinical officers (first 35% of anterior chamber exams supervised by UK ophthalmologist); retinal images graded by 2 independent masked UK ophthalmologists with specialist interest in medical retina; third consultant as arbitrator
caveat: Case-control, 82 survivors; symptomatic-referral; original timing (1-6m) was wrong.
author: Steptoe

rob_detail.notes: Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be NOS; apply the proper NOS instrument

label: S37 · Tozay 2020
timing: Baseline mean 408 days post-ETU discharge; quarterly visits over approximately 3 years
caveat: Symptom questionnaire, no ophthalmologist.
author: Tozay

label: S38 · Van Nguyen 2023
timing: Post-hoc analysis of EVICT study charts; timing relative to acute EVD not specified in abstract
examiner: Not specified (medical chart review of EVICT study data)
caveat: Conference abstract of the Steptoe 2017 EVICT case-control; examiner not stated.
author: Van Nguyen

label: S39 · Vandy 2018
timing: Campaign March 2015 to March 2016; retrospective programmatic data
caveat: Programmatic cohort, 2667; mixed ophthalmologist/mid-level examiners.
author: Vandy

label: S40 · Vandy 2016
timing: March 7 to April 24, 2015 (baseline examination)
caveat: Port Loko 277 (overlaps S20); uveitis reported per eye.
author: Vandy

label: S41 · de Vet 2018
timing: January 2015 to March 2017; visits ranged from 0 to 30 months post-ETU discharge, with 60% at 6–18 months and 30% at ≥24 months
examiner: Non-specialist (ELWA clinic clinicians; slit-lamp examination dependent on availability of trained clinicians)
caveat: General survivor clinic chart review; slit-lamp use variable.
author: de Vet

label: S42 · Eghrari 2015
timing: Post-acute; median 338 days (IQR 293–385) from symptom onset to enrollment; mean additional 100 days (SD 94) from enrollment to eye examination
caveat: PREVAIL III eye substudy (564 survivors); overlaps S54.
author: Eghrari

label: S43 · Hartley 2024
timing: Post-acute; patients identified March 2015–March 2016; screened June 2016–August 2017; surgical outcomes reported at 6 and 12 months post-MSICS
caveat: EVICT cataract-surgery case series (34 operated); surgical selection.
author: Hartley

rob_detail.notes: Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be MINORS; apply the proper MINORS instrument

label: S44 · Mwanza 2024
timing: Post-acute; mean 4.6 months ± 1.8 SD (range: 24 days to 8.5 months) from EVD symptom onset to evaluation
examiner: Ophthalmologist (visiting international and trained DRC national ophthalmologists)

author: Mwanza

label: S45 · Mwanza 2025
timing: Long-term (>2y)
caveat: DRC 2018-20 case-control (120 survivors); independent of EVICT/PREVAIL.
author: Mwanza

rob_detail.notes: Scored with JBI 9-item items but tool should be NOS; apply the proper NOS instrument

label: S46 · Muwonge 2025
timing: Multiple timepoints
caveat: Published Muwonge 2025 report (BMC Medicine) of the Uganda-2022 SUDV cohort; no eye exam. Duplicate preprint ("Severe long-term", S47) removed from corpus. DRC "2/237" data once attributed here belonged to Mwanza 2024 (S44).
author: Muwonge

label: S48 · Kaweesa 2026
timing: Long-term (>2y)
examiner: Trained study nurses and medical officer
caveat: Bundibugyo (BDBV) 2007–08 cohort at ~16–17y; 40 survivors; self-report symptoms only (vision problems 22.5%), no eye exam. Original "SUDV"/"24 months"/"2022" were wrong; same outbreak as S03 Clark.
author: Kaweesa

rob_detail.notes: Q6/Q7 scored 'No': no ophthalmic examination (self-report/none) — ocular condition not validly/reliably measured

label: S49 · Shantha 2018
timing: Convalescence (Phase 1: June 2016; Phase 2: July-August 2017)
caveat: EVICT; enrolled for severe ocular disease; uveitis 92% = lifetime history, not prevalence.
author: Shantha

label: S50 · Berry 2021
caveat: Sierra Leone EVICT-screening (125 pts/250 eyes); original "BDBV/Uganda/16y" note was mis-copied.

label: S51 · Li 2018
timing: Survivor (>6m)
caveat: Commentary/narrative review (Li 2019); no original data.

label: S52 · Dyal 2023
timing: Median 898 days (range 622–1309) after ETU discharge or acute EVD; specimens collected 2017–2018
caveat: All-male semen-persistence study; ocular findings secondary (tonometry performed).

label: S53 · Eghrari 2016
timing: Baseline evaluation (timing from EVD not stated in abstract)

label: S54 · PREVAIL III Study Group 2019
timing: Median 358 days (IQR 313–405) after symptom onset for baseline visit
